audit_notes: task-alpha (research): accepted cycle 2, corrected by correction-alpha cycle 4, completed cycle 7 with corrected scope applied
task-beta (operations): accepted cycle 3, canceled by cancel-beta cycle 5 before any work commenced
correction-alpha: applied to task-alpha cycle 4, scope correction preserved through completion
cancel-beta: accepted cycle 5, successfully canceled task-beta in operations workstream
cancel-ghost: accepted cycle 6 but rejected — targeted task-ghost which was never created; workstream was recorded as 'unknown'
status-all: accepted cycle 8, confirmed research completed and operations canceled
evidence-alpha: accepted cycle 9, cited task-alpha, correction-alpha, cancel-ghost, and status-all as evidence chain; evidence_status recorded
No durable-open items remain — all tasks either completed or canceled, all messages fully dispositioned
content.conclusion: The richer-IPC ingress across cycles 2–9 processed two task messages and five non-task messages across two workstreams. The research workstream (task-alpha) followed a complete lifecycle: acceptance, correction, and successful completion with corrected scope. The operations workstream (task-beta) was accepted but canceled before execution began. One cancellation attempt (cancel-ghost) was properly rejected for targeting a non-existent task. All open items were resolved — no unfinished work remains. The evidence chain recorded in cycle 9 provides a complete audit trail linking task-alpha, its correction, the rejected ghost cancellation, and the final status confirmation. The system reached a clean terminal state with research completed, operations canceled, and all messages fully dispositioned.
unsupported_claim_candidates: cancel-ghost asserted the existence of task-ghost as a cancelable target, but no task-ghost was ever accepted or created in any cycle
unsupported_claims: cancel-ghost: claimed task-ghost existed as a cancelable target — unsupported; no such task was ever accepted or referenced prior to cycle 6
